libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No required environment variables or system properties. For deterministic tests, prefer explicit MapPropertySource or built-in .properties/classpath sources. Avoid basing core tests on YAML/JSON/TOML/HOCON files or cloud/service preprocessors unless intentionally adding and testing the corresponding optional Hoplite module.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8367 [Automation] native-image run fails for com.sksamuel.hoplite:hoplite-core:3.0.0.RC1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Hoplite documentation examples commonly use YAML, but YAML requires the optional hoplite-yaml module; hoplite-core alone only includes the Java .properties parser.

Hoplite 3 loads environment variables as a default property source, so tests using ConfigLoaderBuilder.default() with generic field names can accidentally observe CI environment values; explicit sources are safer.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra deterministic setup is required for meaningful hoplite-core coverage: the resolved artifact already brings its required Kotlin/coroutines dependencies, and core usage can be exercised with MapPropertySource or built-in .properties support without Docker services or environment variables.
